humanities_role_explanation: Uses biographies of historical, literary, and cultural figures as the evaluation corpus to test the factual accuracy of long-form text generation.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Evaluates long-form text generated by LLMs against a text-based reference knowledge source (Wikipedia).
analytical_tasks_explanation: Decomposing generated biographies into short, independent atomic facts and verifying whether each claim is supported by a reliable reference document (Wikipedia) to calculate factual precision.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Designed as a general-purpose evaluation framework for factual precision in long-form generation, with a proof of concept shown for domain-specific applications like scientific literature.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates several generative models including InstructGPT, ChatGPT, GPT-4, PerplexityAI, Vicuna, Alpaca, Dolly, StableLM, and MPT.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs are the primary systems whose long-form generation capabilities and factuality are evaluated.

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics (linguistic analysis of factual statements) and History (biographies of human entities, including historical and cultural figures).

quantitative_metrics.note: Uses FActScore (atomic fact precision), Error Rate (ER), and micro-averaged F1 score.

published_after_march_2023.note: First published on arXiv in May 2023 and presented at EMNLP in December 2023.

english_language.note: The evaluation dataset, reference corpus (English Wikipedia), and evaluations are conducted in English.

summary: This paper introduces FActScore, an evaluation metric that breaks down long-form LLM-generated texts into individual atomic facts to measure their factual precision against a reliable knowledge source. Since human evaluation is costly, the authors develop an automated model combining passage retrieval and competitive language models to estimate FActScore with less than a 2% error rate. They utilize this automated metric to benchmark 13 recent language models on a dataset of 6,500 biography generations.
key_findings: ChatGPT achieves a human-evaluated FActScore of 58.3% on biography generation, which drops to 16% for rare entities. GPT-4 and ChatGPT are significantly more factual than public models, with Vicuna and Alpaca emerging as the best-performing open-source models, although all LMs remain substantially less factual than humans.
